reasoning: Without access to real-time search, I must rely on a base-rate and contextual analysis. The question asks whether Victor Ponta will win the 2025 Bucharest mayoral by-election following Nicușor Dan's departure. Historically, Bucharest's electorate leans center-right, making it difficult for PSD candidates to win unless the center-right is heavily divided (as happened in 2016). Victor Ponta, a former PM who resigned following the 2015 Colectiv tragedy protests in Bucharest, carries very high negative ratings among the capital's voters. Even if PSD or another left-leaning coalition nominates him, he would face an uphill battle against any credible center-right candidate (e.g., from USR/United Right or PNL). Furthermore, it is not guaranteed he would even secure the nomination over established local figures like Daniel Băluță or Gabriela Firea. In prediction markets with multiple candidates, a polarizing figure with uncertain nomination status typically trades at a low single-digit probability. Therefore, I estimate his chances of winning at around 6%.
